items.text: Confirm docs/specpad/ holds specpad.proj.json, specpad.srs.json, and specpad.vtp.json, and that each parses as JSON.
items.expected: All three files exist under docs/specpad/ and parse without error.

items.text: Run validate() on a document missing schemaVersion and on one with an unknown type.
items.expected: Each is reported as structurally invalid (non-empty error list).

items.text: Run validate() on a project document that has name and title.
items.expected: validate() returns no errors.

items.text: Run validate() on an SRS item missing text, and on one missing id.
items.expected: Both are reported invalid, each error naming the missing required field.

items.text: Run validate() on a VTP item with result "maybe" and one with result "passed".
items.expected: The "maybe" item is invalid (result enum); the "passed" item is valid.

items.text: Create an item with the factory, then serialize and parse a document; compare item ids before and after.
items.expected: createSrsItem assigns an id, and the load/save round-trip preserves every id unchanged.

items.text: Generate ids with generateId and inspect ID_PREFIX.
items.expected: Each id matches prefix_<6 hex>, and ID_PREFIX maps requirement→r, test→t, heading→h.

items.text: In the VTP editor, set a test's verifies to a requirement and inspect the stored value.
items.expected: The verifies entry equals the requirement's id, not its code label.

items.text: Rename a requirement's code label and re-run governance on a test that verifies its id.
items.expected: The id is unchanged and referential integrity still holds (verifies targets the id, not the code).
items.notes: src/shared/__tests__/governance.test.ts (code-rename / id-immutability case)

items.text: Render the SRS table for a requirement with one, then two, verifying tests and inspect the saved file.
items.expected: The displayed count is computed from verifies (1, then 2); no numTests field is written to disk.

items.text: Inspect a saved SpecPad document for history fields.
items.expected: No modifiedUser, modifiedDate, or change-list keys are present; the file validates.
items.notes: src/shared/__tests__/dogfood.test.ts (validate)

items.text: Call validate() with documents of each type.
items.expected: validate() selects the schema by doc.type and returns [] for each valid document.

items.text: Validate a structurally-correct SRS+VTP pair that has an untested requirement.
items.expected: validate() returns no errors (policy is not its concern); the traceability gap is found only by governance.
items.notes: src/shared/__tests__/validate.test.ts + governance.test.ts separation

items.text: Run checkGovernance on an SRS+VTP pair where one requirement has no verifying test.
items.expected: A traceability violation is reported for the untested requirement.

items.text: Run checkGovernance where a test verifies a non-existent requirement id.
items.expected: A referential-integrity violation is reported for that test.

items.text: Run checkGovernance on a non-heading test whose expected value is empty.
items.expected: A missing-expected violation is reported for that test.

items.text: Run checkGovernance on a bundle whose only untested requirement is a heading.
items.expected: No traceability violation is reported for the heading.

items.text: Compare the shared module's GOVERNANCE_RULES ids against the skill's SKILL.md.
items.expected: Every governance rule id appears in SKILL.md (parity); drift fails the test.

items.text: Round-trip an SRS document through serialize/parse and exercise the fallback download path.
items.expected: The document round-trips with a trailing newline; a fallback open/download path exists for unsupported browsers.

items.text: Render the SRS table with a VTP that verifies a requirement and read the Tests column.
items.expected: The verifying-test count is shown and updates as more tests verify the requirement.

items.text: In the Testing view, change a test's result and save.
items.expected: onSave receives a VTP document with the updated result on the correct item.

items.text: Render the footer status bar for a clean bundle and for one with an untested requirement.
items.expected: The status bar shows "No problems found" when clean and a traceability message when a requirement is untested.
items.notes: src/components/__tests__/StatusBar.test.tsx (validation surfacing moved from the former ValidationPanel to the StatusBar)

items.text: Render the VTP table with a test whose verifies points at an unknown id.
items.expected: The offending row is visually flagged (danger styling).

items.text: Open a project with two documents and switch between them in the picker.
items.expected: The editor re-seeds the table for the newly selected document; it does not write the prior document's content.

items.text: Follow SKILL.md to scaffold docs/specpad/ from the templates and add items.
items.expected: The folder is scaffolded with PROJECT_NAME replaced and newly added items receive unique stable ids.
items.notes: Manual dogfood; this project's docs/specpad/ was produced this way.

items.text: Run validate() and checkGovernance() over SpecPad's own SpecPad documents.
items.expected: validate() returns no errors and checkGovernance() returns no violations for the SpecPad docs.

items.text: Inspect the generated launcher index.html.
items.expected: It redirects to https://specpad.com/v01/?name=...&open=... using the project name.
items.notes: docs/specpad/index.html; template at skill/specpad/templates/index.html

items.text: Compute buildRedline(baseline, working) with an added and a modified item, then render the SRS table with the resulting redline.
items.expected: Added rows are flagged, modified rows are flagged with the changed cell highlighted, and removed items are listed in a panel.

items.text: Diff two document versions where a code label is renamed and items are reordered.
items.expected: No spurious add/remove; a renamed code is a single modified entry; pure reordering yields no change.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the change-tracking section.
items.expected: It states the skill never computes diffs and that the editor computes all change views (one diff implementation).

items.text: Run the skill refresh against a tagged repo and inspect docs/specpad/.specpad/.
items.expected: The cache holds verbatim snapshot copies plus a release manifest; no computed-diff files are written.
items.notes: Manual dogfood: docs/specpad/.specpad/ (baseline + snapshots); skill/__tests__/change-tracking.test.ts

items.text: Run validate() on a releases manifest and exercise cachedReleases().
items.expected: The manifest validates with each release carrying version, ref, date, and an author {name,email}; cachedReleases maps the baseline release to the baseline location.

items.text: Run computeAttribution over ordered snapshots, then format with attributionLabel.
items.expected: Each item resolves addedIn/lastChangedIn with the release author, and the oldest cached snapshot is marked as a boundary.

items.text: Render VersionTimeline with a manifest of two releases.
items.expected: Both versions render with their dates and authors, and the baseline release is flagged.

items.text: Set a job in JobControl and inspect SKILL.md for the trailer convention.
items.expected: JobControl reports the entered job id and title to its handler; SKILL.md documents folding the current job into a Job: commit trailer; no job field is stored on items.

items.text: Open a project whose change-tracking cache is absent (loaders return null).
items.expected: The editor renders and remains editable, showing a "history unavailable — run specpad refresh" note instead of redline/attribution.
items.notes: LocalApp degraded path: loaders return null when the cache is absent (src/__tests__/LocalApp.test.tsx mocks them null); an explicit banner assertion is still pending.

items.text: Refresh a tagged repo, then pull an older version's snapshot on demand.
items.expected: Refresh caches the latest release snapshot; an older snapshot is written to the cache only when explicitly pulled.
items.notes: Manual dogfood: baseline cached by refresh, v0.1 pulled on demand; skill/__tests__/change-tracking.test.ts documents pull.

items.text: Open a requirement's hamburger menu and choose Add ▸ Child.
items.expected: A new requirement is inserted directly below at level + 1.

items.text: Render the SRS table containing a heading that has a code segment.
items.expected: The heading's Code cell shows the derived dotted path; the literal word "heading" never appears.

items.text: Open a row's hamburger menu.
items.expected: It lists add (above/below/child), add heading, indent/outdent, delete, and view information; delete confirms; outdent is disabled at level 0.

items.text: Click the show-tests toggle on a requirement that has a verifying test.
items.expected: The verifying VTP tests are listed inline beneath the requirement.

items.text: Render the SRS table and open a requirement's information modal.
items.expected: No Hazards column is present in the table; the info modal shows the item's hazards.

items.text: Build the redline rows for a baseline vs a working doc with an added, a modified, and a removed item.
items.expected: Added/modified rows are flagged and the removed row renders inline struck-through at its baseline position; no separate removed panel.
items.notes: src/__tests__/changeTracking.test.ts (buildRedlineRows); src/components/__tests__/SRSTable.test.tsx

items.text: Drag a requirement row onto another position.
items.expected: The items reorder to reflect the drop position.
items.notes: Manual; HTML5 draggable rows in SRSTable; the reorder index math was reviewed but has no DOM drag test.

items.text: Render the editor menu bar with a project open.
items.expected: It shows the SpecPad brand and the primary controls (File, Save, Job, version) in one bar; no separate page header/toolbar.

items.text: Open the File menu and choose New document; render with FS access unavailable.
items.expected: New document fires; with no FS access the menu offers the upload/download fallback open.

items.text: With two projects in the directory, pick the other from the brand dropdown.
items.expected: The chosen project's documents load.

items.text: Render the view tabs with only an SRS document present.
items.expected: Requirements is active; tabs for absent documents are disabled and do not switch when clicked.

items.text: Render the status bar for a clean bundle and for one with a governance violation.
items.expected: Clean shows "No problems found"; a violation shows an error/warning count that expands to the detail messages on click.

items.text: Click the menu-bar version chip and render the version-history dialog.
items.expected: The chip triggers the dialog; the dialog lists releases, or shows an empty state prompting specpad refresh.

items.text: Edit a row in each table and inspect the change callback.
items.expected: Each table calls onChange with the updated document and renders no internal Save button.
items.notes: src/components/__tests__/SRSTable.test.tsx; VTPTable.test.tsx; TestingView.test.tsx

items.text: Render the menu bar clean and then dirty.
items.expected: Save is disabled when clean and enabled with an unsaved indicator when dirty; clicking it saves.
items.notes: src/components/__tests__/MenuBar.test.tsx (Ctrl/Cmd-S wired via the LocalApp keydown effect)

items.text: Switch documents with no pending edits.
items.expected: No prompt appears on a clean switch; a dirty switch prompts before discarding (beforeunload guard exercised manually).
items.notes: src/__tests__/LocalApp.test.tsx (asserts no confirm on a clean switch)

items.text: Open the editor with ?demo and confirm the SpecPad demo project loads with no folder picker, the SRS/VTP/Testing views are populated, and version history opens.
items.expected: The demo project loads read-only over HTTP from /demo/; all read views (tables, testing, validation, version history, redlines, job chip) function.
items.notes: src/__tests__/LocalApp.demo.test.tsx; src/__tests__/localFileApi.demo.test.ts; testing view, version history, and redlines exercised manually in the dev server

items.text: In demo mode, confirm no Save button or File menu is offered, Ctrl+S does nothing, and the read-only indicator is shown.
items.expected: All write affordances are absent or inert and a "Demo — read-only" indicator is visible.

items.text: Run the site build and test suite; confirm every schema field carries a description and the reference HTML includes every field and governance rule.
items.expected: site/src/__tests__/generate-reference.test.ts and src/shared/__tests__/schema-descriptions.test.ts both pass; the build fails if any schema property lacks a description.
items.notes: site/src/__tests__/generate-reference.test.ts; src/shared/__tests__/schema-descriptions.test.ts — automated coverage confirmed in the marketing-site branch test run

items.text: After a deploy, fetch https://specpad.com/specpad-skill.zip and confirm it is a valid zip containing the skill directory rebuilt from the repository.
items.expected: The URL returns a non-empty zip archive whose contents match the skill/ directory at that commit; infra/deploy.sh step rebuilds dist-site/specpad-skill.zip from the repo on every ship.
items.notes: Manual verification 2026-06-10: fetched https://specpad.com/specpad-skill.zip after shipping (application/zip); unzipped cleanly to specpad/SKILL.md + 5 template files matching skill/specpad/. The zip is rebuilt fresh by infra/deploy.sh Ship 4/5 on every deploy.

items.text: Run validate() on a jobs register and on records missing required fields.
items.expected: A well-formed jobs doc validates; a record missing status or title, or with a status outside open/closed, is reported invalid.

items.text: Assert the jobs-register record schema exposes only record fields, no change associations.
items.expected: A job record's schema properties are exactly id/code/title/description/status — no field listing changed items or commits; SKILL.md documents deriving job → SRS → VTP → source from git.
items.notes: src/shared/__tests__/sidecars.test.ts (records-only assertion); docs/design §13.1; SKILL.md on-demand reports.

items.text: Normalize markers via activeJobIds (jobs array and legacy job) and inspect SKILL.md trailer guidance.
items.expected: activeJobIds returns the jobs array when present, falls back to a single job, else []; SKILL.md states one Job: trailer is written per active id, carrying the record id.
items.notes: src/shared/__tests__/governance.test.ts (activeJobIds); sidecars.test.ts (jobs array); skill/__tests__/change-tracking.test.ts (Job: trailer)

items.text: Run checkGovernance with an active marker pointing at a closed record and at an id absent from the register.
items.expected: active-job-open is reported for the closed entry and active-job-known for the dangling entry; open, known entries produce no violation; with no register, neither rule fires.

items.text: Render the Jobs view and the active-job picker; add a job, edit it, toggle the active set, and inspect closed records.
items.expected: The view lists/creates/edits records and multi-selects active jobs; a closed job is never offered for activation; a closed-but-active job can be removed.

items.text: Select a closed job in the Jobs view and attempt to activate it.
items.expected: No activation control is offered for a closed, non-active job; the guidance directs creating a new job; the skill follows the same rule.
items.notes: src/components/__tests__/JobsView.test.tsx (closed job offers no activation)

items.text: Push a commit that has no Job: trailer through the pre-push hook.
items.expected: The hook exits non-zero and blocks the push, naming the offending commit; a commit carrying a Job: trailer passes.
items.notes: .githooks/pre-push (installed via core.hooksPath); skill/specpad/templates/hooks/pre-push; skill/__tests__/pre-push.test.ts

items.text: Push a commit that changes code but touches no SRS/VTP, with and without a Spec: none trailer.
items.expected: The hook warns (without blocking) and recommends a requirement audit; a Spec: none trailer suppresses the warning. When committing via Claude, the agent proposes a requirement derived from the diff for ratification.
items.notes: skill/__tests__/pre-push.test.ts (warn + Spec: none cases); SKILL.md commit workflow Layer 2 (requirement audit)

items.text: Run the pre-push hook over synthetic commits in a throwaway repo: no-trailer, trailer+spec, code-without-spec, Spec: none, tag push, and SPECPAD_SKIP.
items.expected: Block on the no-trailer commit; pass on trailer+spec; warn on code-without-spec; no warning with Spec: none; tag pushes (refs/tags/*) are skipped; full bypass with SPECPAD_SKIP=1. Merge commits and pre-adoption history are not policed.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the SpecPad working-loop section.
items.expected: It documents capturing requirements as you build, spec-first, with the job → requirement → test → code chain attributed to the active job.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the distillation guidance.
items.expected: It states 'intent, not transcript', defines what rises to a requirement (testable behavioral 'shall' statements, not implementation detail), and gives the regression-test litmus.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the autonomous-but-visible capture rule, and confirm this feature was itself captured that way.
items.expected: SKILL.md states requirements are written autonomously then surfaced for the user to edit; JOBS-10..12 were drafted spec-first under JOB-1 with verifying tests (self-dogfood).
items.notes: skill/__tests__/working-loop.test.ts; this feature was captured via the loop it defines.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the init procedure and confirm the artifacts it installs exist.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents the four init steps (scaffold, launcher, hook via core.hooksPath, CLAUDE.md wiring); templates/hooks/pre-push and templates/CLAUDE.specpad.md both exist in the skill.

items.text: Check the CLAUDE directive template for an idempotency sentinel and SKILL.md for the safe-rerun rules.
items.expected: templates/CLAUDE.specpad.md carries the <!-- specpad:working-loop --> sentinel; SKILL.md says to append only when the sentinel is absent and not to overwrite existing docs or clobber an existing core.hooksPath.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for how init handles an existing CLAUDE.md.
items.expected: SKILL.md states init appends the directive and never overwrites CLAUDE.md, creating it only if absent.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the baseline-generator procedure.
items.expected: It documents drafting an initial SRS/VTP from an existing codebase, surveying public surfaces, modules, docs, and existing tests.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the draft/ratification rule.
items.expected: It states output is a draft for human ratification, never authoritative; generated items are tagged `draft`; deriving from code does not bypass user confirmation.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the distillation altitude and governance-clean rule.
items.expected: It states requirements are at the "shall" altitude (not implementation detail) and the output is governance-clean (verifying VTP entry + expected per requirement).

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for test mapping and gap handling.
items.expected: It states requirements map to existing tests (named in notes, result reflecting them) and a behavior with no test is recorded as not_tested rather than omitted.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the coverage-reporting rule.
items.expected: It states the generator reports what it covered and could not cover, with no silent truncation.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the requirement-audit procedure.
items.expected: It documents reconciling the SRS/VTP against the whole codebase in both directions, and notes the commit-time audit is the staged-diff form.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the missing-requirement finding.
items.expected: It states code behavior with no requirement is flagged (missing) and a draft requirement plus test is proposed, derived from the code, for ratification.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the stale-requirement finding.
items.expected: It states a requirement whose behavior is gone/changed is flagged stale for update/removal and never silently deleted.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the coverage-gap finding.
items.expected: It states a requirement with no covering test, or a VTP notes reference to a test that no longer resolves, is flagged.

items.text: Inspect SKILL.md for the report/non-destructive rule.
items.expected: It states findings are categorized proposals to ratify, nothing destructive is applied automatically, and coverage/confidence is reported (no silent truncation).

items.text: Open a project, reopen it from the Recent projects list without re-picking, and forget it.
items.expected: A previously opened folder is listed and reopens via its persisted directory handle with no folder picker; Forget removes it from the list.

items.text: Validate a job record carrying type and version, and one with a bad type.
items.expected: A record with type feature/bugfix and a version validates; a record with an out-of-enum type is rejected; the record schema exposes type and version alongside the existing fields.

items.text: Render the Jobs view with jobs across versions and types.
items.expected: Jobs are grouped by major version (unversioned under Unreleased) with Features and Bugfixes subheadings, and each job's description shows beneath its title.

items.text: Click a job in the Jobs list and then go back.
items.expected: Clicking a job opens a separate detail view (record editor, no list groups); a back control returns to the list.

items.text: Render the view tabs.
items.expected: Jobs renders in the oversight group (with Releases and Auditor), to the right of Overview and the authoring views — not leftmost.

items.text: Confirm job caches exist and the skill documents before-at-create / after-at-close.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents the .specpad/jobs/<id>/{before,after} cache (before written at job create, after at close); committed before/after SRS snapshots exist and validate.
items.notes: skill/__tests__/change-tracking.test.ts (docs + dogfood cache existence/validation)

items.text: Validate a job record carrying an owner, and confirm owner is part of the record schema.
items.expected: A record with owner {name,email} validates; the record schema exposes owner alongside the other record fields.

items.text: Confirm the skill documents deriving version from the release tag containing the job.
items.expected: SKILL.md states version is derived (not hand-set) via git tag --contains at refresh; Unreleased until a release contains the job.

items.text: Render the Jobs view and open a closed job's detail.
items.expected: Open jobs appear under In progress and closed under Released; a closed job's detail renders its cached SRS/VTP changes; the active open job's detail renders its in-progress diff.

items.text: Confirm the skill documents the source-traceability export.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents deriving a job's commits + code changes from git (git log --grep on the Job: trailer), generated on demand, with no maintained matrix.

items.text: Confirm closed-job commit lists are cached and documented.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents writing .specpad/jobs/<id>/commits.json on close; committed commits.json files exist for closed jobs and parse as a list of {hash,subject,author,date}.

items.text: Open a closed job's detail with cached commits.
items.expected: The detail lists the job's commits (hash, subject, author, date) under a Commits heading.

items.text: Confirm the architecture spec is documented and present.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents the <name>.sad.md (arc42) and <name>.workspace.dsl (C4) tracked files; SpecPad's own specpad.sad.md and specpad.workspace.dsl exist and are non-empty.

items.text: Render the Architecture view with an arc42 doc and a C4 DSL.
items.expected: The arc42 headings/content render, and the C4 Structurizr DSL is presented with a link to render it; an empty state shows when absent.

items.text: Confirm the skill documents job/release coupling for architecture.
items.expected: SKILL.md states architecture is job/release-coupled and that no requirement-to-architecture trace matrix is maintained.

items.text: Confirm the skill documents snapshotting architecture into the caches on close.
items.expected: SKILL.md states the SAD/DSL are snapshotted into the release baseline and per-job cache on close/refresh.

items.text: Confirm both architecture profile templates ship.
items.expected: templates/sad.generic.md and templates/sad.medical.md both exist; the medical one has Safety classification & segregation and Architecture Verification sections, the generic one does not.

items.text: Confirm the init quiz is documented.
items.expected: SKILL.md's init procedure asks medical vs generic and scaffolds the matching sad/workspace/guide; generic is the default.

items.text: Confirm the generic template is fuller with multiple views and no classification.
items.expected: The generic SAD has the arc42 sections and no safety classification; the workspace template defines multiple C4 views (systemContext + container).

items.text: Confirm per-profile authoring guides ship, are read by the skill, and render in the editor.
items.expected: templates/sad.guide.generic.md and sad.guide.medical.md exist; SKILL.md says the skill reads the guide before editing; the Architecture view renders a provided guide in a panel.

items.text: Render the Architecture view with and without edit callbacks.
items.expected: Edit/Display sub-tabs appear when editing is enabled; no sub-tabs (display only) when read-only.

items.text: Render Display with arc42 markdown and a diagram SVG.
items.expected: The markdown renders as HTML headings/content (react-markdown) and the diagram SVG renders inline.

items.text: Edit the arc42 markdown in the Edit tab and save.
items.expected: The CodeMirror editor emits changes via onChange; the shell marks the SAD dirty and writes it via saveProjectText on save.
items.notes: src/components/__tests__/ArchitectureView.test.tsx (onChange); src/LocalApp.tsx save path (saveProjectText)

items.text: Inspect the skill docs for diagram change-tracking granularity.
items.expected: SKILL.md states diagram change tracking is coarse (file changed), not in-diagram deltas, with draw.io SVG as the diagram source.

items.text: Confirm the SAD places multiple diagrams via image references.
items.expected: SpecPad's specpad.sad.md references at least four diagrams (context, building block, runtime, deployment) via ![..](*.svg), and those SVG files exist.

items.text: Render the Architecture Display with a markdown-placed diagram and a diagrams map.
items.expected: The referenced diagram SVG renders inline at its position in the document.

items.text: Confirm the C4 DSL is optional in the editor and absent from the dogfood/default.
items.expected: The Architecture view shows the C4 DSL section only when a DSL is present; SpecPad's docs/specpad has no workspace.dsl while the template keeps one for opt-in.

items.text: Load project assets through a fake directory handle: a nested reference, a missing file, and a reference with an invalid name.
items.expected: loadProjectText resolves a subdirectory reference (diagrams/context.svg) by walking into the folder, and returns null (never throws) for a bare missing file, a reference into a missing subdirectory, and a reference the handle rejects with a Name-is-not-allowed error.

items.text: Confirm closed-job caches snapshot the architecture files and a per-state manifest.
items.expected: Each closed job has before/after arch-files.json; a job that created the SAD (JOB-9) shows it added (before manifest empty, after lists specpad.sad.md).

items.text: Open a closed job that changed architecture and view its detail.
items.expected: An Architecture changes section lists added/modified/removed arch files; an SAD markdown change shows an added/removed-line diff.

items.text: Confirm both skills ship — core specpad and the specpad-medical add-on.
items.expected: skill/specpad/SKILL.md and skill/specpad-medical/SKILL.md both exist; the add-on declares it extends core and is opt-in.

items.text: Confirm core carries no medical content and the add-on carries the medical profile.
items.expected: Core's init points the medical path at specpad-medical (not a core medical template); the medical SAD template + guide live under skill/specpad-medical/templates; core's generic template has no classification sections.

items.text: Confirm the add-on does not fork the contract or editor.
items.expected: The specpad-medical skill is prose + templates (no src/); its SKILL.md states the contract and editor are one shared codebase, never a code fork.

items.text: Confirm a release maps to its jobs and the skill documents it.
items.expected: releases.json lists v1.2; the closed jobs carry version v1.2 (so the release groups them); SKILL.md states the release→jobs mapping is derived from job.version.

items.text: Confirm the release baseline snapshots all key doc types.
items.expected: The .specpad/baseline snapshot contains not only proj/srs/vtp JSON but also the SAD (specpad.sad.md) and a diagram SVG; SKILL.md's refresh says to snapshot all key documents.

items.text: Render the Releases view with releases and versioned jobs.
items.expected: Each release shows newest-first with version/date/author and its jobs grouped Features/Bugfixes; closed jobs with no version appear under Unreleased; open jobs are excluded.

items.text: Confirm an optional PRD register is supported as an SRS-shaped peer document.
items.expected: validate() accepts a well-formed prd document (type "prd", id/text-required items) and rejects one missing items; the project index accepts a prd document reference; createPrdDoc/createPrdItem build a prd doc and p_-prefixed items.

items.text: Confirm an SRS requirement traces upward to PRD items via satisfies (by id).
items.expected: SrsItem carries an optional satisfies string array; the srs schema documents it; governance flags a satisfies entry that does not resolve to a PRD item id (prd-referential-integrity).

items.text: Confirm PRD governance is enforced only when a PRD register is present.
items.expected: With a PRD register, an unresolved satisfies raises prd-referential-integrity and an unsatisfied implemented PRD item raises prd-coverage; with no PRD register, no prd-* violations are raised even when SRS items carry satisfies.

items.text: Confirm PRD item status gates coverage (proposed/roadmap is exempt).
items.expected: A PRD item with status "implemented" and no satisfier raises prd-coverage; items with status "proposed" or no status raise nothing; createPrdItem defaults new items to "proposed"; the status enum rejects values outside {proposed, implemented}.

items.text: Render the Auditor view as an orientation index.
items.expected: The Auditor tab renders the scope statement, the design-control map, and the roadmap, with links into the relevant tabs; it does not itself render the trace matrix.

items.text: Confirm the Traceability view reports requirements, test results, and PRD coverage.
items.expected: buildAuditReport returns verified/total requirements, passed/failed/not-tested test counts, and implemented-vs-satisfied plus proposed PRD counts; the Traceability view shows them.

items.text: Confirm the Traceability matrix links PRD→requirement→test with results and flags unverified requirements.
items.expected: Each trace row resolves a requirement's satisfied PRDs and verifying tests with a result rollup; a requirement with no verifying test is rolled up as no_test and visibly flagged in the Traceability tab.

items.text: Confirm the gap list (Traceability) and the roadmap (Auditor).
items.expected: The Traceability view lists checkGovernance violations (and shows governance-clean when none); the Auditor view lists the proposed PRD items as roadmap.

items.text: Confirm the Auditor view shows the scope disclaimer.
items.expected: The view renders text stating SpecPad produces evidence supporting design controls and is not a QMS / compliance determination.

items.text: Confirm the editor classifies and loads a PRD register.
items.expected: classifyDocFilename recognizes <name>.prd.json as a prd document; loadPrd reads it; the Auditor tab is available when an SRS is open.

items.text: Render the design-control map with citations, statuses, and links.
items.expected: The Auditor view lists the formal elements (Design Inputs, Outputs, Verification, Validation, Traceability, Design Changes, Design History File, Design Reviews, Risk Management, Configuration Management), each with an IEC 62304 / 21 CFR 820.30 citation, a statement, a status badge, and a navigation link where applicable.

items.text: Confirm element status is derived from the project.
items.expected: buildDesignControls marks Design Inputs present when requirements exist, Design Verification by coverage, Design Changes present when jobs exist, Design History present when releases exist, and Design Validation / Risk Management as gaps.

items.text: Confirm the visual system is token-based.
items.expected: specpad.less defines CSS custom properties (design tokens) on :root for color/surface/type/spacing, and components reference var(--token) rather than hard-coded values; each theme overrides the same token set.

items.text: Confirm theme selection persists and applies without flash.
items.expected: applyTheme sets data-theme on the document root and writes localStorage; readStoredTheme returns the saved value (default when unset); the three themes are offered; index.html sets the saved theme before the app mounts.

items.text: Confirm the grouped tab order leads with Overview.
items.expected: ViewTabs renders Overview first, then the authoring group (Requirements, Verification Tests, Results, Architecture), then the oversight group (Auditor, Releases, Jobs), with a separator between groups.

items.text: Render the Overview with status, latest release, and coverage headline.
items.expected: OverviewView shows the open jobs, the latest release, and a coverage/health headline (verified count and governance findings), with buttons that navigate to the authoring views.

items.text: Confirm Overview is the default view on open.
items.expected: After opening a project, the active view is Overview (the editor's currentView initializes to overview).

items.text: Confirm per-doc-type authoring guides ship and are referenced, not inlined.
items.expected: skill/specpad/guides/{requirements,tests,product-requirements,architecture}.md exist; SKILL.md references each by path; SKILL.md does not inline a guide body (it stays a routing pointer).

items.text: Confirm each guide covers what-to-capture and good/bad examples.
items.expected: Each guide file contains a what-to-capture section and clearly-marked good and bad examples (e.g. ✅/❌ or Good/Bad headings).

items.text: Confirm SKILL.md and the guides stay in sync.
items.expected: The set of guide files under skill/specpad/guides/ equals the set referenced by SKILL.md — no guide is unreferenced and no referenced guide is missing.

items.text: Confirm the skill snapshots before at job creation.
items.expected: SKILL.md's job procedure states that the before snapshot (.specpad/jobs/<id>/before/) is written when a job is created, not only on close; the active job's before snapshot exists and validates.

items.text: Render the active open job's in-progress changes.
items.expected: Given an activeDiffs entry for the active open job (before vs working), the Jobs view detail renders its in-progress SRS/VTP changes instead of the 'materialized once closed' message.

items.text: Render the active open job's in-progress architecture changes.
items.expected: Given an activeArch entry for the active open job, the Jobs view detail renders an Architecture changes section (added/modified/removed files + SAD line diff) for the open job, not only for closed jobs.

items.text: Confirm the document-type registry describes each content type.
items.expected: DOC_TYPES includes srs, vtp, and prd as register kinds, each with a schema, a label, optional/inBaseline flags, and a generate setting; REGISTER_TYPES filters to register kinds.

items.text: Confirm validation and the schema reference are built from the registry.
items.expected: validate() accepts every register type in DOC_TYPES plus the sidecars (project/releases/job/jobs); the generated reference includes each registry type. Adding a type to the registry makes validate() accept it with no edit to validate.ts.

items.text: Confirm the skill snapshots/caches every document in the project index.
items.expected: SKILL.md's refresh and job-cache prose say to snapshot every document in the project index (documents[]) plus the architecture files, iterating the index rather than a fixed list.

items.text: Confirm snapshot/diff are document-type-generic (extensibility).
items.expected: classifyDocFilename recognizes a register type from the registry; a synthetic register type added to the registry is diffed by the shared diffItems and flows through the per-job diff path with no code change; the Jobs view renders a DiffList per register type present.

items.text: Render and edit the Product Requirements tab with status and redline.
items.expected: PrdTable renders the PRD items with an inline status dropdown; changing status/text/tags calls onChange with the updated doc; added/modified rows since the baseline are highlighted (redline); add/delete controls add and remove items; the tab is enabled only when a PRD register is present.

items.text: Confirm the baseline generator drafts PRD + a starter SAD by default (registry-aware).
items.expected: SKILL.md's baseline generator states it drafts the full default set — a PRD (proposed, with SRS linked via satisfies) and a starter arc42 architecture — in addition to SRS/VTP, is registry-aware, and that a type may be declined.

items.text: Confirm init scaffolds the full default set including the PRD.
items.expected: SKILL.md's init scaffolds the full default set; starter.proj.json lists a prd document; a starter.prd.json template ships and is a valid empty PRD; createProjectDoc's default documents are prd/srs/vtp.

items.text: Confirm the PRD tab shows each item's downward trace and flags gaps.
items.expected: PrdTable's Satisfied-by column lists the SRS codes that satisfy each PRD item; a proposed item reads as roadmap; an implemented item with no satisfier is flagged as a gap.

items.text: Confirm a design-control phase band spans the right tabs.
items.expected: ViewTabs renders a phase band per design-control phase; the Design Inputs band spans the Product Requirements + Requirements columns (grid-column 2 / span 2); bands cover Design Verification, Outputs, Controls, Traceability, History, Changes.

items.text: Confirm the working loop directs a job to evaluate impact across every registered document type (not just SRS/VTP).
items.expected: SKILL.md and the CLAUDE.specpad.md template both instruct evaluating the job's impact on every registered document type — naming the PRD (user-facing intent) and the architecture/SAD (structural changes) alongside SRS/VTP — before implementing, spec-first.

items.text: Confirm architecture is a registered (prose) document type.
items.expected: docTypeFor('sad') is defined with kind 'prose' and no schema; 'sad' is absent from REGISTER_TYPES (not diffed as an id-keyed register) yet present in DOC_TYPES (an impactable type).

items.text: Confirm PRD and VTP tables use the SRS interaction model (hamburger + expandable relationship toggle + info-dialog history).
items.expected: PrdTable and VTPTable each render a per-row 'Row actions' hamburger; PRD shows an expandable 'Satisfied by' toggle and VTP an expandable 'Verifies' toggle (collapsed by default); VTP per-item history (attribution) appears in the info dialog, not a column.

items.text: Confirm the PRD table hides Status/Tags and flags proposed items.
items.expected: PrdTable renders no Status control and no Tags column; a proposed item shows a single bold 'PROPOSED' note and its text is italicised; implemented items show no such note.

items.text: Confirm the Verification Tests view shows no execution Result.
items.expected: VTPTable renders no 'Result' column/control; results remain editable only on the Results (TestingView) tab.

items.text: Confirm the software-requirements view is labelled 'Software Requirements'.
items.expected: ViewTabs renders the tab as 'Software Requirements'; the doc-type registry label for srs is 'Software Requirements'; SRSTable's heading falls back to 'Software Requirements'.

items.text: Confirm the document tabs are ordered chronologically by design-control phase.
items.expected: ViewTabs renders tabs in the order Overview, Product Requirements, Software Requirements, Architecture, Verification Tests, Results, Auditor, Traceability, Releases, Jobs.

items.text: Confirm a modified markdown architecture file gets a section-aware diff and diagrams are coarse.
items.expected: mdSectionDiff reports only changed sections by heading with added/removed lines; the Jobs view renders each modified .md file's changed section headings, and reports a changed diagram coarsely as 'diagram changed'.

items.text: Confirm the active job's in-progress architecture diff is section-aware for markdown.
items.expected: The active open job's in-progress architecture changes render the modified SAD's changed section headings and their added/removed lines (section-aware), built from mdSectionDiff over the before snapshot vs the working SAD.

items.text: Confirm the skill documents the release-cut job closing itself as the final step before the release.
items.expected: SKILL.md's 'Cutting a release' procedure states the release-cut job closes itself (status closed) as the last step before the tag, that its version derives to that release, and that an open release-cut job after the release ships is a process error.

items.text: Confirm VTP items carry framework-agnostic automation linkage that validates.
items.expected: A VtpItem with automation [{runner,file,selector}] validates; a RunRecord validates as a sidecar; the matcher resolves links by file/selector.

items.text: Confirm a normalized run record is defined and validates.
items.expected: validate() accepts a run sidecar with runner/ref/ranAt/summary/results and rejects a malformed summary; every run-schema field has a description.

items.text: Confirm automated results derive from the run (and manual falls back).
items.expected: verificationOutcome derives passed/failed/not_run for automated items from the run and ignores a stale stored result; a manual item falls back to its result; rollup counts by status.

items.text: Confirm the Results view groups Manual/Automated tests, shows click-to-edit automation links + a derived status, and omits the verifies trace.
items.expected: TestingView renders a Manual section above an Automated section; an automated test shows its automation link read-only (click reveals inputs) and a run-derived status badge; no Verifies column is present.

items.text: Confirm the skill documents capturing runs and freezing them for deliverables.
items.expected: SKILL.md documents the VTP->test->run chain: the automation linkage, a normalized run sidecar from a machine reporter (framework-agnostic), result derivation, and freezing the run into the release baseline and each closed job's after cache.

items.text: Confirm the run-detail dialog lists the captured run's per-test outcomes.
items.expected: Opening 'View run output' shows the run summary and a per-test table (file, selector, status, duration) with a failures-only filter.
items.notes: run-detail dialog

items.text: Confirm a job's detail shows the run-derived verification status of its changed tests.
items.expected: JobsView's job detail renders a Verification section summarizing the run-derived status (passed/not-run/fail) of the tests the job added/modified, using the job's frozen run or the latest run.

items.text: Confirm roll-ups derive automated results from the run, not a stored field.
items.expected: buildAuditReport derives test pass/fail from the run for automated tests (and falls back to a manual test's stored result); the Overview headline shows the run-derived passing count.
